LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS. TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS AWARDED $4776 AS REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS, CALCULATED AT $150 PER HOUR FOR 30 HOURS AND $276 IN COSTS. 30 HOURS IS A REASONABLE NUMBER OF HOURS FOR THE WORK PERFORMED. $150 PER HOUR IS A REASONABLE HOURLY RATE FOR ROUTINE COLLECTION WORK. 30 HOURS IS A REASONABLE NUMBER OF HOURS FOR THE WORK PERFORMED. IF DEFENDANT SEEKS ANY FURTHER FEES AND COSTS, SHE NEEDS TO SHOW THAT SHE MADE A REASONABLE SETTLEMENT DEMAND CONSISTENT WITH PLAINTIFF'S DOCUMENTED ABILITY TO PAY AND THAT THE DEMAND WAS REJECTED. LAW AND MOTION, PLAINTIFF GELAREH RAHBAR'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF JAN. 12, 2011. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND TO VACATE PRIOR RULING IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008 OR CCP 473. PLAINTIFF DID NOT DEMONSTRATE ANY DILIGENCE, OPPOSE THE MOTION, OR CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF THE MOTION AND HEARING. FURTHER THERE WAS NO MISTAKE AT LAW. FILING A REPETITIVE SLAPP COMPLAINT AND THEN DISMISSING IT IN AN UNTIMELY FASHION IS NOT A REASONABLE MISTAKE. IN ADDITION, THE COURT RULED: 1ST - RE: PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT THE COURT LACKED JURISDICTION TO HEAR OR CONSIDER SLAPP MOTION FOLLOWING PLAINTIFF'S DISMISSAL - A DEFENDANT WHO IS VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED WITH OR WITHOUT PREJUDICE, AFTER FILING AN [ANTI-SLAPP MOTION], IS NEVERTHELESS ENTITLED TO HAVE THE MERITS OF SUCH MOTION HEARD AS A PREDICATE TO A DETERMINATION OF THE DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS WHICH A COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION TO CONSIDER. SEE WONG V. TAI JING (2010) 189 CAL.APP.4TH 1354 CITING TO LIU V. MOORE (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 745, 751; KYLE V. CARMON (1999) 71 CAL.APP.4TH 901, 917-919. 2ND - COURT FINDS THE HOLDING IN S.B. BEACH PROPERTIES V. BERTI (2006) 39 CAL.4TH 374 SUPPORTS DEFENDANT'S ARGUMENT THAT POLICY CONSIDERATIONS FAVOR ALLOWING DEFENDANT TO FILE HER ANTI-SLAPP MOTION EVEN BEFORE SHE WAS SERVED WITH COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302)
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-14-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Motion For Post Judgment Atty Fees
Off Calendar Feb-28-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON'S Ntc Of Motion For Post Judgment Atty Fees is Granted, as set forth herein. Defendant is entitled to attorney fees for the appeal. The billing rates for the appeal are reasonable. Defendant is awarded $51,997 in attorney fees, and $621.10 in costs, for a total amount of $52,618.10 for attorney fees and costs in connection with the appeal. The Defendants billing rates for routine collection work is excessive. For this work, defendant may recover $150 hour. There were approximately 28 hours of routine collection work, for a total of $4200. For the motion to amend the judgment and for preparation of this motion for fees, Defendants' proffered billing rates are reasonable.
For the motion to amend judgment, Defendant sought $23,888. This appears excessive, including excessive hearing time, and three attorneys working on the reply papers. The Court awards $20,000. For the fee motion, Defendant sought $20,322.50. This appears excessive for the preparation of an additional fee motion in the same case. The Court awards $12,500.
The Court awards $2,681.45 in costs/expenses. Grand total amount of fees and costs is $91,999.95. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Joanne Bryce, CSR # 3321 =302/mjm

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The weight of the evidence shows that there is a unity of interest between Dr. Rahbar and Rahbar Dentistry, P.C. and that maintaining the fiction of a separate existence of them promotes injustice. Motion And Motion For Atty'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Clifford, Esq., of California Anti-Slapp Project, 510-486-9123 for defendant and moving party

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Granted in part. Defendant is awarded $4776 as reasonable fees and costs, calculated at $150 per hour for 30 hours and $276 in costs. 30 hours is a reasonable number of hours for the work performed. $150 per hour is a reasonable hourly rate for routine collection work. 30 hours is a reasonable number of hours for the work performed. If defendant seeks any further fees and costs, she needs to show that she made a reasonable settlement demand consistent with plaintiff's documented ability to pay and that the demand was rejected. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR 7077

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Jan 12, 2011 Ruling
Off Calendar Apr-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF GELAREH RAHBAR'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF JAN. 12, 2011 RULING.

APPEARANCES: Paul J. Steiner, Esq. of Law Offices of Paul J. Steiner 981-6100 for pltf & moving party; Mark Goldowitz, Esq. of California Anti-SLAPP Project 510/486-9123 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION AND TO VACATE PRIOR RULING IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP 1008 OR CCP 473. PLAINTIFF DID NOT DEMONSTRATE ANY DILIGENCE, OPPOSE THE MOTION, OR CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF THE MOTION AND HEARING. FURTHER THERE WAS NO MISTAKE AT LAW. FILING A REPETITIVE SLAPP COMPLAINT AND THEN DISMISSING IT IN AN UNTIMELY FASHION IS NOT A REASONABLE MISTAKE.
In addition, the Court ruled:
1st - Re: plaintiff's argument that the Court lacked jurisdiction to hear or consider SLAPP Motion following plaintiff's dismissal - a defendant who is voluntarily dismissed with or without prejudice, after filing an [anti-SLAPP motion], is nevertheless entitled to have the merits of such motion heard as a predicate to a determination of the defendant's motion for attorney fees and costs which a court retains jurisdiction to consider. See Wong v. Tai Jing (2010) 189 Cal.App.4th 1354 citing to Liu v. Moore (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 745, 751; Kyle v. Carmon (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 901, 917-919.
2nd - Court finds the holding in S.B. Beach Properties v. Berti (2006) 39 Cal.4th 374 supports defendant's argument that policy considerations favor allowing defendant to file her Anti-SLAPP Motion even before she was served with complaint.
Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order.
(302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JENNIFER BATOON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied as moot in light of the December 27, 2010 dismissal. Defendant, however, has shown that the suit was a SLAPP and may file a motion to recover fees per CCP 425.16(c). (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
